Notice Of Motion And Motion Bycarnival Corporation For An Orderfinding Plaintiff To Be A Vexatiouslitigant, For Security Andalternative Dismissal, And For Pre-Filing Order
Off Calendar Jun-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Motion By Carnival Corporation for An Order finding Plaintiff To Be A Vexatious litigant, For Security And alternative Dismissal, And For Pre-Filing Order.

Appearances: Philip Barilovits, via Zoom, Kaye, Rose & Partners, LLP, (619) 232-6555, for Defendant Carnival Corporation.

Motion not argued/contested. The court notes Plaintiff's absence at this hearing. The court also notes that this matter has apparently settled and acknowledges that a Request for Dismissal has been filed. Counsel confirms the preceding and advises that the intent was to notify the court of the settlement prior to today, but that the parties had been finalizing the settlement during the past weekend. The court notes for the record that it is considering, on its own motion, entering a prefiling order in this matter, pursuant to CCP 391.7.

The Motion By Carnival Corporation for An Order finding Plaintiff To Be A Vexatious litigant, For Security And alternative Dismissal, And For Pre-Filing Order is off calendar.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR: 11185, T: (415) 551.0334, E: adelgado@sftc.org. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION
Off Calendar May-09-2023 Off Calendar

Off Calendar May-09-2023 Off Calendar

Notice That Motion By Carnival Corporation For An Order Finding Plaintiff To Be A Vexatious Litigant, For Security And Alternative Dismissal, And For Pre-Filing Order Has Been Taken Off Calendar For July 15, 2022 And Re-Noticed For July 25, 2022
Off Calendar Feb-28-2023 Continued To May-15-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CARNIVAL CORPORATION Notice That Motion By Carnival Corporation For An Order Finding Plaintiff To Be A Vexatious Litigant, For Security And Alternative Dismissal, And For Pre-Filing Order Has Been Taken Off Calendar For July 15, 2022 And Re-Noticed For July 25, 2022

Appearances (by Zoom):
Elizabeth Karnazes, 650-345-9200, Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Philip Barilowitz, Esq. Anita Eilert. Esq. 619-232-6555, Kaye Rose & Partners LLP for Defendant CARNIVAL CORPORATION
Close Captioning by Kitty Baca, kittybaca@kittybaca.com, 415-279-7195.
Court reporter: For the record.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. the Coourt announces there is no stay since the Presiding Judge denied the request to stay. Plaintiff informs the Court she will appeal the ruling and has 2 surgeries scheduled. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CARNIVAL CORPORATION'S Motion For An Order Finding Plaintiff To Be A Vexatious Litigant, For Security And Alternative Dismissal, And For Pre-Filing Order Has Been Taken Off Calendar For July 15, 2022 And Re-Noticed For July 25, 2022.

Continued to 5/15/23 at 9:30am. The Court sets a briefing schedule. Plaintiff's Opposition is due by - 5/1/23 and the hearing on the motion is set for 5/15/23 at 9:30am in Dept. 302. Mr.Barilovitz to re-serve Ms. Karnazes with all motions. Mr. Barilowitz Meyers to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: For the Record, Reported. (302/RBU)

Plaintiff ELIZABETH KARNAZES (Tel: 650-533-3456) in pro per;

Notice That Motion By Carnival Corporation For An Order Finding Plaintiff To Be A Vexatious Litigant, For Security And Alternative Dismissal, And For Pre-Filing Order Has Been Taken Off Calendar For July 15, 2022 And Re-Noticed For July 25, 2022
Off Calendar Jul-25-2022 Continued To Feb-01-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff ELIZABETH
Attys. ANITA M. EILERT, Esq. and PHILIP BAROLOVITS, Esq. of Kaye, Rose & Partners, LLP (Tel: 619-232-6555) for defendant Carnival Corporation.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CARNIVAL CORPORATION's Motion For An Order Finding Plaintiff To Be A Vexatious Litigant, For Security And Alternative Dismissal, And For Pre-Filing Order.

Matter argued.

The Court notes Judge Ulmer signed the order granting plaintiff's ADA request, and so any request by defendant for reconsideration of that order must be brought by a noticed motion for a hearing date in which Judge Ulmer is presiding over the Law & Motion Calendar.

The Court adupts its tentative ruling.

Matter is continued to February 1, 2023. Matter is stayed through Jan. 31, 2023 in light of plaintiff's ADA request.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/CK)
